problem.title: Flip Game II
problem.description: You are playing Flip Game with your friend. Given a string s containing only '+' and '-', a player can flip two consecutive '++' into '--'. The player who cannot make a move loses. Return true if the starting player can guarantee a win with optimal play.
assumptions: A player loses when they cannot make any move.

example.explanation: Starting player flips positions 1-2: '--++'. Opponent must flip 3-4: '----'. Starting player wins (opponent has no moves).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Recursive Backtracking [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Try every valid move from the current position and recursively check whether the opponent loses (canWin returns false for the opponent). If any move leads to the opponent losing, the current player wins. The key insight is that a position is a win if and only if there exists at least one move that puts the opponent in a losing position.

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Memoization [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Cache each string state in a HashMap so repeated positions are not recomputed. This dramatically reduces redundant calls when the same board state can be reached via different move orderings.
